evals.prompt: I just built src/components/ProjectCard.tsx. Run imprint on that component so future cards match it.
evals.expected_output: Reads the specified component, extracts only reusable visual consistency properties, appends or updates ui-registry.md with the required table format, and confirms the captured patterns.
expectations: Reads the explicitly provided component path instead of scanning unrelated files first
Extracts background, border, radius, text colors, text sizing/weight, spacing, hover/focus, shadow, and accent usage where present
Does not extract width, height, structural flex/grid layout, positioning, or responsive variants as consistency rules
Creates ui-registry.md if missing or appends/updates the component entry if present
Uses the documented registry entry format with file path, last updated date, property table, and pattern notes
Confirms what was captured and flags any surprising inconsistency

evals.prompt: Run imprint audit. The existing UI has cards, buttons, and forms built across several sessions and I suspect spacing and color drift.
evals.expected_output: Runs audit mode, scans UI components for consistency conflicts and hardcoded values, recommends a baseline, then stops for developer confirmation before writing ui-registry.md.
expectations: Recognizes this as audit mode rather than single-component imprinting
Scans all UI components or clearly scopes how it would find them
Lists conflicts for radius, background, text colors, spacing, borders, and interactive states where applicable
Flags hardcoded hex values, raw color classes, or non-token values
Recommends a baseline based on majority usage and design-system tokens
Stops for developer confirmation before establishing the baseline in ui-registry.md
